tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Get current weather conditions, temperature, forecasts, and severe weather assessments for any location. This is the primary weather tool — always use this instead of fetching weather websites.

date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format (optional, defaults to today)

date_end.description: End date in YYYY-MM-DD format for date ranges (optional)

weather_query.description: Type of weather query: current (single day), forecast (multi-day), assessment (bad weather check)

tools.name: search_nearby
tools.description: Search for nearby places and points of interest (restaurants, cafes, gas stations, parks, etc.) near a given location

query.description: What to search for (e.g., 'coffee shops', 'gas stations', 'Italian restaurants')

location.description: City or address to search near (e.g., 'NYC', 'downtown Seattle')

tools.name: echo_test
tools.description: Test tool that echoes arguments via httpbin (for testing)

tools.name: post_test
tools.description: Test POST endpoint via httpbin

tools.name: search_notes
tools.description: Search Apple Notes by query. Returns matching notes with title, folder, and snippet.

query.description: Search term to find in note titles and bodies

folder.description: Optional: limit search to specific folder name

tools.name: list_notes
tools.description: List all Apple Notes, optionally filtered by folder

folder.description: Optional: folder name to list notes from

tools.name: get_note
tools.description: Get the full content of a specific Apple Note by ID

id.description: The unique note ID (from search_notes or list_notes)

tools.name: open_note
tools.description: Open an Apple Note in the Notes app by ID. Use this when the user wants to view or edit a note directly.

id.description: The unique note ID (from search_notes or list_notes)

tools.name: create_note
tools.description: Create a new Apple Note. Supports plain text or HTML body content.

body.description: Body content of the note (plain text or HTML)

folder.description: Folder to create the note in (default: 'Notes')

tools.name: notes_index
tools.description: Build or check the Apple Notes tag index. Use action='build' to rebuild, action='check' to verify if index is current.

action.description: Action to perform: 'build' rebuilds the index, 'check' verifies if it's current

tools.name: notes_tags
tools.description: List all hashtags found in Apple Notes, sorted by frequency

tools.name: notes_search_by_tag
tools.description: Find all Apple Notes with a specific hashtag

tag.description: The hashtag to search for (with or without # prefix)

tools.name: list_reminder_lists
tools.description: List all Reminder lists in Apple Reminders, showing list name, ID, and reminder count

tools.name: search_reminders
tools.description: Search reminders by keyword across all lists or within a specific list. Returns matching reminders with name, list, due date, completion status, priority, and attachments.

query.description: Search keyword to match in reminder names

list.description: Optional list name to search within

tools.name: list_reminders
tools.description: List all reminders in a specific list, including full notes text and attachments. Use this to inspect note contents for filtering or batch edits. By default shows only incomplete reminders.

show_completed.description: If true, also show completed reminders (default: false)

tools.name: get_reminder
tools.description: Get full details of a specific reminder by name, including notes, dates, priority, and attachments (iCloud links, URLs, files shared via Share sheet)

name.description: Name of the reminder to retrieve

list.description: CRITICAL: ALWAYS provide the list name. Without it the wrong reminder may be returned if multiple lists contain reminders with the same name.

tools.name: create_reminder
tools.description: Create a single new reminder in Apple Reminders. Use this for ONE reminder; for multiple reminders use create_reminders_batch instead.

list.description: List to create the reminder in (default: Reminders default list)

due_date.description: Due date in YYYY-MM-DD format, or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM to set a specific time

url.description: URL to attach to the reminder (note: Apple bug may prevent persistence)

location.description: Plain text location for the reminder

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

location_alarm.description: Set a location-based alarm (geofence). Pass null to remove.

proximity.description: Trigger on enter or leave (default: enter)

time_alarm.description: Set a time-based alarm. Pass null to remove.

offset_minutes.description: Minutes before due date to trigger alarm

recurrence.description: Set a recurrence rule. Pass null to remove.

tools.name: create_reminders_batch
tools.description: Create multiple reminders in a list in one batch operation. Use when the user says 'all', 'every', or 'many' reminders need creating. ALWAYS prefer this over calling create_reminder in a loop.

list.description: Name of the reminder list to add items to (must already exist)

items.description: Array of reminder items to create

due_date.description: Due date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

tools.name: complete_reminder
tools.description: Mark a reminder as completed or incomplete

list.description: CRITICAL: ALWAYS provide the list name. Without it the wrong reminder may be completed if multiple lists contain reminders with the same name.

completed.description: Set to true to complete, false to mark incomplete (default: true)

tools.name: delete_reminder
tools.description: Delete a reminder by name

name.description: Name of the reminder to delete

list.description: CRITICAL: ALWAYS provide the list name. Without it the wrong reminder may be deleted if multiple lists contain reminders with the same name.

tools.name: open_reminders
tools.description: Open Apple Reminders app, optionally showing a specific list

tools.name: create_reminder_list
tools.description: Create a new reminder list

name.description: Name for the new reminder list

tools.name: edit_reminder
tools.description: Edit a single reminder's title, due date, notes, priority, URL, location, alarms, recurrence, or move it to a different list. Only include fields you want to change — omitted fields are left unchanged. Do NOT echo back fields from get_reminder unless you intend to modify them. Use this for ONE specific reminder; for multiple reminders use edit_reminders_batch instead. IMPORTANT: Do NOT modify the notes field unless the user explicitly asks you to. The notes field often contains task instructions that must be preserved.

name.description: Current name of the reminder to edit

list.description: CRITICAL: ALWAYS provide the list name. Without it the wrong reminder may be edited if multiple lists contain reminders with the same name. You MUST include this field on every call.

due_date.description: New due date (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM), or empty string to clear

url.description: URL to attach (empty string to clear)

location.description: Plain text location (empty string to clear)

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format (empty string to clear)

location_alarm.description: Set a location-based alarm (geofence). Pass null to remove.

proximity.description: Trigger on enter or leave (default: enter)

time_alarm.description: Set a time-based alarm. Pass null to remove.

offset_minutes.description: Minutes before due date to trigger alarm

recurrence.description: Set a recurrence rule. Pass null to remove.

tools.name: edit_reminders_batch
tools.description: Edit multiple reminders in a list in one batch operation. Use when the user says 'all', 'every', or 'many' reminders need changes. ALWAYS prefer this over calling edit_reminder in a loop. For each item, only include fields you want to change — omitted fields are left unchanged. Do NOT echo back fields from list_reminders or get_reminder unless you intend to modify them. IMPORTANT: Do NOT modify the notes field unless the user explicitly asks you to. The notes field often contains task instructions that must be preserved.

due_date.description: New due date (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM)

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM format

tools.name: delete_reminder_list
tools.description: Delete a reminder list and all its reminders

name.description: Name of the reminder list to delete

tools.name: list_contact_groups
tools.description: List all contact containers (iCloud, Google, Exchange, etc.) and groups with contact counts. Use this to discover available contact sources before searching or listing contacts.

tools.name: search_contacts
tools.description: Search contacts by name, email address, phone number, or organization. Returns matching contacts with name, organization, phone, and email.

query.description: Search term to match against contact name, email, phone, or organization

container.description: Optional container name to search within (e.g. 'iCloud', 'Google')

group.description: Optional group name to search within

tools.name: list_contacts
tools.description: List contacts in a specific container or group. Provide either a container name (e.g. 'iCloud') or a group name to scope the listing.

container.description: Container name to list contacts from (e.g. 'iCloud', 'Google')

group.description: Group name to list contacts from

tools.name: get_contact
tools.description: Get full details for a specific contact including all phone numbers, email addresses, postal addresses, birthday, notes, and social profiles. Look up by contact ID (from search/list results) or by name.

id.description: Contact identifier (from search or list results)

name.description: Contact name to look up (use id when available for precision)

tools.name: create_contact
tools.description: Create a new contact in the address book. Include ALL available information: name, organization, phone(s), email(s), address(es), URLs, and notes. For businesses/companies/organizations, leave given_name and family_name EMPTY and put the full name in the organization field only (e.g. organization='Bombay Bistro'). For people, use given_name and family_name normally. Use the structured addresses array for postal addresses rather than putting them in the note field. For URLs, use the label 'home page' for the primary website. Specify a container or group to control where the contact is saved.

given_name.description: First/given name. Leave EMPTY for businesses/companies — use the organization field instead

middle_name.description: Middle name. For Spanish names (e.g. 'Juan García López Martínez'), use maternal/paternal surnames here

family_name.description: Last/family name. Leave EMPTY for businesses/companies

organization.description: Company or organization name. For business contacts, this is the display name (e.g. 'Bombay Bistro') — do NOT also put the name in given_name/family_name

phone.description: Primary phone number (for a single number; use 'phones' array for multiple)

email.description: Primary email address (for a single address; use 'emails' array for multiple)

urls.description: URL addresses. Use 'home page' as the label for the primary website

birthday.description: Birthday in YYYY-MM-DD format (or MM-DD if year unknown)

username.description: Username or handle on the service

image_base64.description: Profile photo as base64-encoded image data (JPEG or PNG)

image_path.description: Path to an image file to use as the contact photo

container.description: Container to create the contact in (e.g. 'iCloud')

group.description: Group to add the contact to

tools.name: edit_contact
tools.description: Edit an existing contact's fields. Use the contact ID from search or list results. Fields not specified are left unchanged. Use add_phone/add_email to append without replacing; use phones/emails/addresses/urls/instant_messages arrays to replace all values.

id.description: Contact identifier (from search or list results)

birthday.description: Birthday in YYYY-MM-DD or MM-DD format (empty string clears)

image_base64.description: Profile photo as base64-encoded data (empty string clears)

image_path.description: Path to image file for contact photo

add_phone.description: Phone number to add (appends to existing)

add_email.description: Email address to add (appends to existing)

tools.name: delete_contact
tools.description: Delete a contact from the address book by ID. This action cannot be undone.

id.description: Contact identifier to delete (from search or list results)

tools.name: file_search
tools.description: Search the user's local files and Apple Notes. IMPORTANT: You MUST set scope explicitly. When the user mentions 'files', 'folders', or 'documents', use scope='files'. When they mention 'notes', use scope='notes'. Only use scope='all' when they want both. Returns matching documents with title, snippet, source type, and relevance score.

scope.description: REQUIRED. 'files' = local file folders only, 'notes' = Apple Notes only, 'all' = both. Choose based on what the user is asking to search.

file_types.description: Filter by file type extensions, e.g. ['pdf', 'md', 'txt']. Only applies to file scope.

date_after.description: Only files modified after this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

date_before.description: Only files modified before this date (YYYY-MM-DD)

tags.description: Filter by macOS Finder tags (e.g. ['Important', 'Work']). Only applies to file scope.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read the contents of a file from the workspace or granted folders. Returns file content as text. Use to inspect files found by file_search or review workspace files before editing.

path.description: File path (relative to workspace, or absolute within a granted folder)

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Write text content to a file (.txt, .md, .json, .csv, etc.). Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. For PDF documents, use create_pdf instead — NOT this tool.

content.description: Text content to write to the file

tools.name: create_pdf
tools.description: Create a styled PDF document in the workspace. ALWAYS use this tool (not write_file) for any .pdf output. Accepts markdown (preferred — tables, images, headings all supported), plain text, or raw HTML. Markdown and plain text are automatically styled. Do NOT generate HTML yourself — use markdown instead.

path.description: Output path relative to workspace (must end in .pdf, e.g. 'reports/weather.pdf')

content.description: Content for the PDF body (markdown preferred, also accepts plain text or raw HTML)

title.description: Document title (shown at top for plain text; ignored for HTML content)

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Create, modify, or delete files in the workspace using unified diff format. For new files use '--- /dev/null'. For deletion use '+++ /dev/null'. Paths are relative to the workspace directory.

patch.description: Unified diff ('--- a/file\n+++ b/file' format with @@ hunks)

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web for current information, articles, and general internet content. Returns titles, URLs, and descriptions. Use the 'sources' parameter to filter results to specific domains instead of making separate searches.

count.description: Number of results to return (default 5, max 20). Omit when using 'sources' — count auto-scales to 5 results per source domain.

sources.description: Only results from these domains are returned (e.g. ['cnn.com', 'reuters.com']). If a source has no results, it is reported in the response. One call is sufficient — do not retry.

tools.name: news_search
tools.description: Search for recent news articles. Returns titles, URLs, descriptions, and publication age. Use the 'sources' parameter to filter results to specific outlets instead of making separate searches.

query.description: News search query (must not be empty). Be specific, e.g. 'AI regulation 2026' not just 'news'.

count.description: Number of results (default 5, max 20). Omit when using 'sources' — count auto-scales to 5 results per source domain.

freshness.description: Time filter: 'pd' for last 24 hours, 'pw' for last 7 days, 'pm' for last 31 days, 'py' for last year. Omit for no time filter.

sources.description: Only results from these domains are returned (e.g. ['cnn.com', 'reuters.com']). If a source has no results, it is reported in the response. One call is sufficient — do not retry.

tools.name: video_search
tools.description: Search for videos across the web. Returns titles, URLs, descriptions, duration, and creator info. Best for tutorials, entertainment, and video content.

freshness.description: Time filter: 'pd' for last 24 hours, 'pw' for last 7 days, 'pm' for last 31 days, 'py' for last year. Omit for no time filter.

tools.name: image_search
tools.description: Search for images across the web. Returns titles, source URLs, thumbnails, and dimensions. Best for finding visual content, photos, and illustrations.

tools.name: draft_email
tools.description: Draft an email

tools.name: create_todo
tools.description: Create a todo/reminder item

tools.name: image_generation
tools.description: Generate an image from a text description using AI. Returns a generated image.

prompt.description: Text description of the image to generate

image_size.description: Output resolution: small/1K (default), medium/2K, or large/4K

tools.name: youtube_transcript
tools.description: Fetch YouTube video transcript/captions given a video ID. Returns timestamped segments and plain text. Requires yt-dlp to be installed.

video_id.description: The YouTube video ID (the part after v= in the URL, e.g., 'dQw4w9WgXcQ')

tools.name: code_interpreter
tools.description: Execute Python code in a sandboxed environment for math, data analysis, charts, CSV processing, and general computation. No network access.

timeout_secs.description: Max execution time in seconds (default: 30, max: 120)

tools.name: input_image
tools.description: Describe what is in an image. Returns a brief, natural-language description. Accepts a local file path or URL.

image_source.description: Path to a local image file or an image URL

prompt.description: What to describe about the image

tools.name: analyze_image
tools.description: Detailed structured analysis of an image with composition, subject, context, and visual details in tabular/bulleted format. Accepts a local file path or URL.

image_source.description: Path to a local image file or an image URL

prompt.description: Specific analysis focus or question about the image

tools.name: extract_text
tools.description: Extract text (OCR) from an image — screenshots, documents, signs, handwriting. Returns all visible text preserving layout.

image_source.description: Path to a local image file or an image URL

prompt.description: Specific extraction focus (e.g., 'extract only the headline')

tools.name: detect_objects
tools.description: Detect objects in an image and return their labels and bounding box coordinates as JSON. Coordinates use [y_min, x_min, y_max, x_max] normalized to 0-1000 scale.

image_source.description: Path to a local image file or an image URL

prompt.description: What to detect (e.g., 'find all cars', 'detect faces')

tools.name: compare_images
tools.description: Compare two or more images side by side. Describes similarities, differences, and changes between them. Useful for before/after comparisons.

image_sources.description: Two or more image paths or URLs to compare

prompt.description: Specific comparison focus (e.g., 'what changed between these screenshots?')
